content: Before running experiments, the agent lays out a justified modeling and investigation strategy: how it will represent the grid (buses, links, generators, demand, wind, storage), what tool/method it will use (e.g. linear optimal power flow via PyPSA), how it will quantify curtailment, and what hypothesis about the curtailment mechanism it intends to test. The plan identifies that wind is concentrated in Scotland while demand is in England across limited boundary links, and frames the curtailment question accordingly. Judged on the soundness of the framing and process design, not on a reference value.
keywords: States a clear modeling approach and tool choice with justification.
Defines a precise, reproducible curtailment metric before running.
Frames an explicit, testable hypothesis about the cause of curtailment.
Recognizes the spatial mismatch (Scottish wind vs English demand vs limited boundary links) as the setup.

content: Baseline characterization: running optimal power flow on the provided system quantifies the wind curtailment (on the order of ~45%, comparable to the reference's ~44.5%), and a constrained-vs-unconstrained comparison shows that lifting transmission limits removes essentially all curtailment. Key Scotland-England boundary links operate at or near 100% loading for much of the week, identifying the transmission bottleneck. This baseline is largely determined by the data and is a low-weight anchor.
keywords: Curtailment rate quantified at roughly 45% under network constraints.
Unconstrained comparison shows curtailment falling to ~0%, isolating constraints as the driver.
Boundary links identified as saturated (near 100% loading) for most hours.
Numerical result broadly consistent with the reference case.

content: The agent designs and runs at least one nontrivial counterfactual/sensitivity experiment that intervenes on the system to test the mechanism - ideally one that pits competing explanations against each other rather than simply turning the obvious knob. Examples: comparing added transmission capacity vs added storage vs demand-side flexibility, removing or scaling a specific bottleneck link, or spatially redistributing wind. Merely scaling line capacity (whose effect is tautological in a linear model) earns little credit; credit is for an experiment whose outcome could genuinely have gone either way. These experiments have no reference ground truth and are judged on experimental design quality (scientific-capability axis).
keywords: At least one counterfactual intervention beyond the constrained/unconstrained baseline.
Experiment is discriminating - it can distinguish competing explanations, not just confirm the obvious.
Intervention is well-controlled (one factor varied, comparable conditions).
Goes beyond tautological line-capacity scaling toward storage/flexibility/spatial alternatives.

content: Using the counterfactual evidence, the agent argues whether the transmission bottleneck is the dominant causal driver of curtailment as opposed to a mere correlate, and reports honestly when an intervention it expected to help (e.g. adding short-duration storage) does not substantially relieve sustained multi-day wind surplus. The causal claim is grounded in the intervention results, not asserted.
keywords: Causal conclusion is supported by the counterfactual/intervention results.
Explicitly distinguishes a causal driver from a correlate.
Honestly reports interventions that fail to help, rather than only confirming evidence.
Weighs competing explanations (transmission vs storage vs flexibility) against the evidence.

content: The agent distinguishes conclusions that are robust to modeling choices from those that are artifacts, e.g. by checking sensitivity to the curtailment metric, time horizon, marginal-cost assumptions, or solver/formulation, and stating which findings survive and which do not.
keywords: Tests sensitivity of the main conclusion to at least one modeling choice.
Separates robust findings from formulation/data artifacts.
States limitations of the one-week, 20-bus setup.
Conclusions are appropriately hedged where the evidence is weak.

content: Reproducible diagnostic outputs support the analysis: e.g. a dispatched-vs-curtailed wind time series over the 168-hour week and boundary-link loading over time, plus clear code/figures for the counterfactual experiments. Judged on whether the diagnostics substantiate the claims.
keywords: Dispatch-vs-curtailment time series produced and readable.
Boundary-link loading diagnostic shown over the simulation period.
Counterfactual experiment outputs are presented clearly.
Analysis is reproducible from the provided code/outputs.
